issue_title: ConditionalOtpFormAuthenticator fails to set CONFIGURE_TOTP required action for LDAP read-only users
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

When LDAP user federation is configured in read-only mode it is not possible to set required actions for users from LDAP. 
Currently, `ConditionalOtpFormAuthenticator` attempts to set the `CONFIGURE_TOTP` required action when a user does not have OTP credentials configured. However, this operation fails with a `org.keycloak.storage.ReadOnlyException`.
Evaluate whether it is possible to add the required action to the authentication session instead of directly to the user, as done in `OTPFormAuthenticator`.


### Version

26.1.3

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

`ConditionalOtpFormAuthenticator` should trigger the `CONFIGURE_TOTP` required action for any user without OTP credentials, even if the user is from an LDAP federation in read-only mode.

### Actual behavior

The required action `CONFIGURE_TOTP` is not triggered by `ConditionalOtpFormAuthenticator` if the user belongs to an LDAP user federation configured in read-only mode.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Setup ldap user federation in read-only mode.
2. Configure custom auth flow with `ConditionalOtpFormAuthenticator`.
3. Try to login with an ldap user without otp credential configured.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Set CONFIGURE_OTP via auth session in ConditionalOtpFormAuthenticator

Closes #37869

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Graziano <g.graziano94@gmail.com>

issue_title: Make JGroups with TLS startup more robust
issue_body:
### Description

With zero-configuration encryption, the certificates are rotated regularly. During this procedure, a node may join the cluster to obtain the old certificate and be unable to join the cluster.

For testing purposes, the certificates are rotated every 5 seconds, and it leaks the short rotation period to the remaining tests. I suspect it is causing failures in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/37798

I cannot claim it fixes all the instability in `Clustering IT`, as those flaky tests happened before this feature was introduced, but it seems to improve it: https://github.com/pruivo/keycloak/actions/runs/13701043754.
commit_message:
Make JGroups with TLS startup more robust

Fixes #37887

Signed-off-by: Pedro Ruivo <pruivo@redhat.com>

issue_title: Unwanted placeholder texts in user profile fields
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

If no placeholder text is specified for user profile fields, the admin UI defaults to trying to resolve a text with the name of the attribute as a key. I think this is a bug, since this floods the UI with placeholder texts that are most often either a) the name of the attribute (which is useless to the end user) or b) the same as the label which already precedes the field in the UI.

Even worse; we have an attribute named "fullName" with no placeholder text defined (we don't want any). Now, placeholder text appears like this:

![Unwanted placeholder text](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f51ab8a0-1e94-4040-aff2-2e7332c55b05)

Since "fullName" key is defined as `{{givenName}} {{familyName}}` in message bundles.

I believe this occurs after fix for #31111, specifically [this commit](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/c8d64f289105c979a99fcbf7fd4b64b976be2805).

### Version

26.0.7

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

If no placeholder text is defined, no placeholder text should be displayed. This is in line with most UX recommendations, e.g. https://uxmovement.com/forms/stop-misusing-placeholder-text-on-textfields/

### Actual behavior

Always displays placeholder text containing either attribute name or a matching message from message bundles.

### How to Reproduce?

Add managed attribute called "fullName" to see the effect.

### Anything else?

The issue #31111 does not seem to intent on introducing this behaviour, as it only relates to `inputOptionLabelsI18nPrefix`
commit_message:
removed fallback to attribute name on placeholder (#38005)

fixes: #37911

Signed-off-by: Erik Jan de Wit <erikjan.dewit@gmail.com>

issue_title: When testing/evaluating permissions UMA resources are not resolved properly
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

When evaluating/testing permissions and sending only the name of the resource in the policy evaluation request, UMA resources are not resolved and the evaluation result does not hold the policies that were executed.

Introduced by https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/37606.

### Version

999.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Return the policies executed when evaluating permissions for UMA resources.

### Actual behavior

When evaluating/testing permissions and sending only the name of the resource in the policy evaluation request, UMA resources are not resolved and the evaluation result does not hold the policies that were executed.

### How to Reproduce?

Send a policy evaluation request for a UMA resource by setting only the resource name and not the ID.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
When testing/evaluating permissions UMA resources are not resolved properly

Closes #37920

Signed-off-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>

issue_title: Custom Authenticator SPI MAP_TYPE default value ignored in Admin UI
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

I have a custom extension that has one configuration property of type `MAP_TYPE` that sets a default value. Since the new admin UI console the default value has been ignored for this property type and just comes up null. 

### Version

v26.1.3

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The default property would be used and appear in the UI.

### Actual behavior

The default value is null so the map property is empty in the UI.

### How to Reproduce?

1) Have a custom authenticator spi with a config property type of `MAP_TYPE` and set a default value to it.
2) In the Admin UI, create a new flow and add the executor of the custom authenticator spi. 
3) Click the gear icon for the configuration settings dialog.
- The `MAP_TYPE` property will be empty and not show the default value. 

### Anything else?

This has been a regression with the new admin UI. It previously worked from Keycloak v11 on until I was forced to use the new admin ui theme. 
Related issues - 
#20235
commit_message:
use default value when specified (#38048)

fixes: #37928

Signed-off-by: Erik Jan de Wit <erikjan.dewit@gmail.com>

issue_title: Inconsistent use of single quotes in message resources
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

### Area

translations

### Describe the bug

In several places (especially in the login theme and the email themes, everywhere there are Freemarker templates, Keycloak uses Java MessageFormat. 

This will remove single quotes, and treat everything within single quotes as escaped. 

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

We shouldn't use single quotes in the message resources, only double quotes. 

### Actual behavior

Single quotes disappear, pleaceholders are escaped.

### How to Reproduce?

Try out MessageFormat

### Anything else?

I'll provide a pull request.

main

I'll create a PR.

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

### Area

_No response_

### Describe the bug

👋 Hello, 

When I create a _(LDAP based)_ User Federation, whether via web admin console or via nodejs admin client, the ID of this freshly created User Federation does not respect the UUID format.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/886df9b5-6d8e-48f4-967c-dcbdcaa18c75)

Consequently, at each authentication of a user coming from this User Federation, a WARN log from `org.keycloak.storage.jpa.KeyUtils` is thrown :
> The given key is not a valid key per specification, future migration might fail

### Version

26.1.3

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The User Federation to have a correct UUID-format compliant id, either using admin WEB console or nodejs admin client to create it.

### Actual behavior

The User Federation ID is for instance :
- Sfze6h46SvmLlM-FyZ5VSw
- fe581sx0TBm6l-Mg2JMHZA
- OjPxlVS-TROZkEmkuGoqEw
- RaQXxaH_SGamVvd-6CBB2w

Either using admin WEB console or nodejs admin client to create it.

And consequently, a WARN log from `org.keycloak.storage.jpa.KeyUtils` is thrown _(certainly at each authentication ?)_ :
> The given key is not a valid key per specification, future migration might fail

It could cause high volume of logs for instances which does a lot of authentications.

### How to Reproduce?

Create a LDAP based user federation via web admin console or nodejs admin client.
When coming back to this freshly created User Federation, you'll see in the URL the ID of the User Federation, not respecting the UUID format.

Reproducing this issue since 26.1.0

It seems we hadn't this issue in Keycloak 24.0.5

I can't say, for now, for Keycloak 25.

Could you please tell me what Area can I set for this issue ?

issue_title: User session limit exceeded for both realm and client removes the wrong session
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When enabling User Session Count Limiter the wrong session is removed if the behavior is set to "Terminate oldest session" and the limit is exceeded for both realm and client at the same time. The oldest session is removed regardless of client.

When session limit for realm is set to 3 and client session limit is set to 1 and a user has 2 active sessions with client-1 and client-2, when the user then creates a new session with client-2 the old session for client-2 is removed regardless of which session is the oldest.

If the a user has 3 active sessions; client-1, client-2 and client-3, and the user creates a new session the oldest session is removed regardless of client. I.e. the user can end up with 3 active sessions; client-1, client-1 and client-3.

### Version

26.1.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

If both realm user session limit and client user session limit exceeds at the same time the oldest session of the same client should be removed first.

### Actual behavior

The oldest user session is removed regardless of client.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a flow with a required "User session count lmiter" step
2. Configure the step to have the "Terminate oldest session" behavior and realm session limit 3 and client session limit 1
3. Create client-1, client-2 & client-3 and configure them to use the authentication flow from above
4. Login on client-1
5. Login on client-2
6. Observe that the user has 2 active sessions
7. Login on client-2
8. Observe that the user has 2 active session (client-1 and client-2)
9. Login on client-3
10. Observe that the user has 3 active session (client-1, client-2 and client-3)
11. Login on client-3
12. Observe that the user has 3 active session (client-2, client-3 and client-3)

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
BUGFIX: session limit exceeded for both client & realm

This commit fixes a bug the wrong user session is removed if the user session limit
for realm and client is exceeded at the same time.

Closes #38016

issue_title: User created with undefined locale except when they explicitely select their language
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

Hello Keycloak team,  

I’d like to highlight an important issue that may require your attention:  

When users create an account in Keycloak, they are not assigned a language unless they explicitly select one from the dropdown on the login page.  

As a result, confirmation and password reset emails are sent in the realm’s default language.  

### Version

Likely all versions, certainly the latest.

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Typically, an application provides the `ui_locales` query parameter when redirecting to the authorization URL.  

If `ui_locales` is not explicitly provided, Keycloak checks the `Accept-Language` header and attempts to match it with an enabled language in the realm. If no match is found, the page is rendered in the realm’s default locale.  

The locale determined through this process should be assigned to the user upon account creation.  

### Actual behavior

Unless the user explicitly selects a language from the dropdown, the account is created without a defined locale, causing emails to be sent in the realm's default language.  

This happens almost every time, as users have no reason to use the language selector when the login page is already displayed in their preferred language—either because the client application set it via `ui_locales` or Keycloak detected it based on the browser's language preferences.  

### How to Reproduce?

You may have your own way of running Keycloak, but here's how to do it using the Keycloakify tooling:  

```bash
git clone https://github.com/keycloakify/keycloakify-starter
cd keycloakify-starter
yarn
npx keycloakify start-keycloak
# Select any Keycloak version
```


1. Enable the default theme (this issue does not occur with the Keycloakify theme, as it has been fixed there).  
    Navigate to [https://localhost:8080](https://localhost:8080) and log in with `admin:admin`.  
3. Select **"myrealm"**.  
4. In the **Themes** tab, enable the default login theme.  
5. Open [https://my-theme.keycloakify.dev](https://my-theme.keycloakify.dev).  
6. Append `&ui_locales=es` to the URL and reload the page.  
7. Create an account.  
8. Go back to the **Admin Console** and check the newly created user—the language has not been set.  

### Anything else?

I fixed this in Keycloakify by making a fetch request to login-action/registrations?kc_locale=xx, but this shouldn’t be necessary.

Wishing you all the best!
commit_message:
Add locale attribute to the registration context
Closes #38029

issue_title: Issue in clearing offline sessions internally using ClearExpiredUserSessions Scheduled task
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

When "clientOfflineSessionIdleTimeout" realm attribute is set with some value > 0, eg: 180 and  "offlineSessionMaxLifespanEnabled": false, Then the refresh_token got for the client/user has no expiration for offline token, which is the expected behaviour based on "offlineSessionMaxLifespanEnabled".

Keycloak internally runs a scheduled task for every 15 minutes to cleanup the expired offline sessions. where it cleans the offline sessions only based on "clientOfflineSessionIdleTimeout", which makes the refresh_token invalid.

Note: In UI we doesn't have support to add "clientOfflineSessionIdleTimeout" but it can be set via update realm API.

### Version

26.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

when the "offlineSessionMaxLifespanEnabled" realm attribute is set to **false** the refresh_token shouldn't be cleared even when the "clientOfflineSessionIdleTimeout" attribute has value.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak internally clears the offline session when "clientOfflineSessionIdleTimeout" has value and expiry is calculated with the value and current time even the "offlineSessionMaxLifespanEnabled" is false.

### How to Reproduce?

- Using update realm endpoint update the "clientOfflineSessionIdleTimeout" to greater than 0 (Eg: 120) and "offlineSessionMaxLifespanEnabled" to false.
- Get an refresh_token using token endpoint and verify the field "refresh_expires_in" has value 0.
- Verify the refresh_token using offline token exchange and it should able to get the new access_token.
- Wait for some time at least for 15 to 30 minutes, where scheduled task to run internally for every 15minutes to clean the offline session based on the "clientOfflineSessionIdleTimeout".
- Then verify the same refresh_token using offline token exchange, it'll throw error as "invalid_grant", "session doesn't have required client".

### Anything else?

_No response_

Added condition to check offlineSessionMaxLifespanEnabled on clearing

Closes #38063

issue_title: Worse UX with new realm selector
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

The newly introduced realm selector has some usability concerns, and honestly doesn't look very polished either.

Some issues I've identified:

* Placement on the top menu is not ideal at least not for my usual flows; my usual flow is to focus on the left hand menu, and with the old placement in the left menu it was quicker to glance at what realm was active, and change the realm is needed. Now I need to focus both my eyes and the mouse on a different place in the UI
* There's a lot of noise as there is now 3 elements (label + dropdown + create realm), this makes it look rather unpolished
* It's very close to and distracts from the Keycloak logo, which visually isn't very pleasing
* With less than 10 realms the drop-down has "Current realm", "Recently used", and the rest. This means I need to look two places when switching to a realm. First looking in recently used, then it all the rest. I don't think there should be recently used at all when there is 10 realms or less, just list the current realm, then the rest
* The dropdown has entries that are multi lines if a realm has display name set. This makes the dropdown awkward as the size of the elements differ. I would rather just show the display name if there is one, otherwise realm name. Alternatively some way of showing both on a single line would be okay
* The dropdown is very narrow if there is no realms with a long display name or realm name; would be good to have a minimum-width set
* Create realm isn't something that is used super frequently; while now it takes up pretty much the most valuable real estate on the UI

### Version

main

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Better UX

### Actual behavior

Worse UX

### How to Reproduce?

Open the admin UI ;)

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Profile Custom Attribute Group: Click on attribute group changes URL, breaking the navigation in AdminUI
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

There seems to be a problem with the user-profile attribute-groups navigation, breaking the browser-back feature, leading the user to an undefined state. Reloading the AdminUI solves the issue.



### Version

26.1.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A click on the group scrolls the group in view. Triggering a browser-back action is going to the previous viewed page/tab or attribute-group. 

### Actual behavior

 If a user is on a user-profile page and clicks on a custom attribute-group (screenshot 1; "Details") the URL is changing (see screenshot 2) and the browser hops to the correct group. If a user is now trying to go back, using the browser-back button, the navigation breaks, leading the user to an invalid page (screenshot 3). From there almost every interaction leads to further errors (screenshot 4). Refreshing the page helps but also sets the user back to the initial page.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/61661cf5-7c48-4780-a693-261c4d965937)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e8bb0091-7a7c-4df3-9342-1638bba9520b)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c18d63dc-e6e9-4e12-963d-c48c5dcbcc61)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f6096d3-b361-496e-84c5-cb6fa979847d)

### How to Reproduce?

- Install Keycloak
- Add Realm
- Add custom profile fields with a custom attribute group
- Go to a realm user
- Click on the non-default attribute group (right hand of attributes)
- Trigger a browser-back action
- Try to navigate away from the page

### Anything else?

The url-change behavior is the same for AdminUI and AccountUI but only the admin-ui navigation is affected by this. In account-ui everything works as expected.

issue_title: Cannot authenticate to "admin-cli" client due to Java null pointer exception
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

After upgrading from Keycloak 25 to Keycloak 26.1.2, the `admin-cli` client in a custom realm (`gp`) became unusable due to a `java.lang.NullPointerException`. This issue prevents me from:
- Obtaining an **access token** via the `/token` oAuth endpoint when using `admin-cli` with `client_id` and `client_secret`.
- Accessing the **Service Accounts Roles** tab for `admin-cli`.
- Making any **modifications** to the client.

All of these actions were possible before my upgrade.

The error **does not occur** in the `master` realm, only in the `gp` realm.

### Version

26.1.2

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The `admin-cli` client in the `gp` realm should:
- Allow authentication via `/token` using `client_credentials` (i.e. client ID and secret). I have the **Client Authentication** checkbox checked on the **General** tab of the client configuration.
- Allow review of the **Service Accounts Roles** tab.
- Enable **saving changes to the client** without errors.

### Actual behavior

- Navigating to the **Service Accounts Roles** tab results in a **Java NullPointerException**.
- Attempting to modify the client configuration results in the same error.
- Authentication using `client_credentials` fails for `admin-cli`.

Error message (from logs):

```
2025-03-16 02:17:07,269 ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-1) Uncaught server error: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot read field "id" because "copy" is null
	at org.keycloak.component.ComponentModel.<init>(ComponentModel.java:51)
	at org.keycloak.component.PrioritizedComponentModel.<init>(PrioritizedComponentModel.java:35)
	at org.keycloak.storage.CacheableStorageProviderModel.<init>(CacheableStorageProviderModel.java:51)
	at org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageProviderModel.<init>(UserStorageProviderModel.java:42)
	at org.keycloak.models.cache.infinispan.UserCacheSession.cacheUser(UserCacheSession.java:353)
	at org.keycloak.models.cache.infinispan.UserCacheSession.getUserAdapter(UserCacheSession.java:309)
	at org.keycloak.models.cache.infinispan.UserCacheSession.findServiceAccount(UserCacheSession.java:540)
	at org.keycloak.models.cache.infinispan.UserCacheSession.getServiceAccount(UserCacheSession.java:500)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.ClientResource.updateClientFromRep(ClientResource.java:809)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.ClientResource.update(ClientResource.java:150)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.ClientResource$quarkusrestinvoker$update_c18fc2ee235fe1638ccf09830bdd635e799b1172.invoke(Unknown Source)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.handlers.InvocationHandler.handle(InvocationHandler.java:29)
	at io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.runtime.QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.invokeHandler(QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:141)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.run(AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.java:147)
	at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder$14.runWith(VertxCoreRecorder.java:635)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.doRunWith(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2516)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2495)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1521)
	at org.jboss.threads.DelegatingRunnable.run(DelegatingRunnable.java:11)
	at org.jboss.threads.ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.run(ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.java:11)
	at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30)
	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1583)
```

### How to Reproduce?

1. **Upgrade** from **Keycloak 25 to Keycloak 26**.
2. In a **custom realm (not `master`)**, navigate to **Clients** and then click the `admin-cli` client.
3. Click on **Service Accounts Roles**. An error message will appear.
4. Observe the **Java error** in the logs.
5. Try to **modify the client confoiguration** and note that the same error occurs.
6. Attempt to **retrieve an access token** using:
   ```
   curl -X POST "https://your-keycloak-server.com/realms/gp/protocol/openid-connect/token" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
        -d "client_id=admin-cli" \
        -d "client_secret=your-secret" \
        -d "grant_type=client_credentials"
```

Note that the request **fails** with `{"error":"unauthorized_client","error_description":"Invalid client or Invalid client credentials"}`.

### Anything else?

- MySQL Server 8.0.35 is hosting the Keycloak database.
- Keycloak is running standalone out of `/opt/keycloak` on Ubuntu Linux (not in a Docker container).
- Service accounts are **enabled** for `admin-cli` in the database (`SERVICE_ACCOUNTS_ENABLED = 1`).
- The `admin-cli` client has a **valid service account user** in the `USER_ENTITY` table.
- The issue **only occurs in the `gp` realm**—the `admin-cli` client in the `master` realm works fine.

issue_title: Managed resource not injected if a dependency is incompatible
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

If a managed resource in the new test framework has a dependency that is destroyed because it is incompatible the registry won't always re-create the resource.

Example where it doesn't work:

```
@InjectChild
Child hasDepedencyOnParent

@InjectParent
Parent parent
```

The registry will list requested instances (hasDepedencyOnParent and parent). It will then match against deployed resources (hasDepedencyOnParent is fine, but parent is not as it's incompatible). Problem is that it now thinks only parent needs creating, and not hasDepedencyOnParent.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Child should be re-created

### Actual behavior

Child is not re-created, but it's destroyed, so we end up not injecting the child.

### How to Reproduce?

Inject a managed child first, then a managed parent second, where the parent is marked as always incompatible. Have two test method and observes that the second test method fails as the child is null.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Managed resource not injected if a dependency is incompatibl (#38196)

Closes #38193

Signed-off-by: stianst <stianst@gmail.com>

issue_title: Injected HttpClient is always re-created
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

HttpClientSupplier implements `compatible` with returning always `false`. This results in re-creating the HttpClient for each test method, but there is no reason not to re-use the client.

Additionally, HttpClientSupplier uses the default lifecycle `CLASS`, but this can be re-used across many tests, so should be `GLOBAL`.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

HttpClient should be re-used

### Actual behavior

HttpClient is never re-used

### How to Reproduce?

A test class with two test methods with HttpClient injected; observe that HttpClient is always re-created even though it should be re-used.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Re-use HttpClient across multiple tests (#38197)

Closes #38195

issue_title: Keycloak rotate certificate without delay when rotation time is less then 100s
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

When generating certificate for jgroups the `validityStartDate` time is set to `currentTime - 100s` in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/be818502ad43e0352acfe6f94082aef4adbdaac0/crypto/default/src/main/java/org/keycloak/crypto/def/BCCertificateUtilsProvider.java#L173

this is causing problems as we are computing next rotation time based on the `validityStartDate` value from the certificate. This means when rotation time is less then 100s we rotate immediately which result in an indefinite rotation loop.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

rotation is scheduled from the time of certificate generation

### Actual behavior

it is not

### How to Reproduce?

Run `JGroupsCertificateRotationClusterTest#testAutoRotation` and see that after this tests the rotation is done too often. You can add `--log-level=org.keycloak.infinispan.module.certificates.CertificateReloadManager` to see more details

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Unexpected transformation of user labels in the Account UI
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/32050 added a translation function around the user labels of a credential. The purpose was to translate `My password`, an hardcoded user label.

As a side effect, user labels manually entered by a user (e.g. for WebAuthn credentials) are now translated. 
**More importantly**, the translation function transforms the label even if no translation is found. Labels can be truncated or non-alphanumeric characters can be swapped with another one.

As an example, `my:label:with:colons` becomes `label.with.colons`.
The `my` has been truncated, and colons `:` are now dots `.`

FYI, the backend is properly storing the user labels, and the Rest API call to fetch all the credentials is showing the right label.


### Version

26.1.4

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

As a user, I expect the label I manually entered to be **exactly the same** when shown on the Account UI.

### Actual behavior

When I enter a label for my credentials, it can be truncated/transformed when shown on the Account UI.  
As an example, `my:label:with:colons` becomes `label.with.colons`.

### How to Reproduce?

You can reproduce the bug by enabling WebAuthn credentials (Passkey) on your realm.

First, the Account Console should shows a section to setup a WebAuthn credentials (Passkey).
- Enable the `WebAuthn Passwordless Register` required action.
- Add the `WebAuthn Passwordless Authenticator` to any of your authentication flows.

When it's done, simply try to create a Passkey on the Account Console. 

When asked to enter a label, enter `my:label:with:colons`. 

Once created, you should see `label.with.colons` instead in your list.

### Anything else?

The bug appears to have been introduced here: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/32050
